forecast-period.text: A ridge of high pressure lies across SA. A cold front will clip the southeast on Tuesday morning, followed by a high pressure system which becomes dominant. The high is expected to move into the Tasman Sea by Friday as a cold front moves across the Bight.
For latest warnings go to www.bom.gov.au, subscribe to RSS feeds, call 1300 659 210* or listen for warnings on relevant TV and radio broadcasts.
* Calls to 1300 numbers cost around 27.5c incl. GST, higher from mobiles or public phones.

Morning fog patches about the agricultural area and in the west. Isolated showers about the agricultural area, contracting to southern coasts in the evening. Maximum temperatures near to below average. South to southwesterly winds, fresh about southern coasts.
Isolated showers about the agricultural area. Dry elsewhere. Southwest to southeasterly winds, tending west to northwesterly about the south in the evening.

Morning fog patches about the agricultural area and in the west. Isolated showers about the agricultural area in the morning, becoming less likely during the afternoon. Maximum temperatures near to below average. Southwest to southeasterly winds tending easterly in the west.
Morning fog patches about the agricultural areas. Mostly dry. Maximum temperatures near to below average. South to southeasterly winds, tending east to northeasterly in the west.

text.p: Morning fog patches about the east and southeast. Isolated showers developing about the northwest during the day. Maximum temperatures near to below average. East to northeasterly winds.
Cumulative rainfall totals to the end of Friday: Dry across the North East Pastoral district. Elsewhere, less than 2 mm, with 2 to 5 mm about southern coasts and ranges and the far northwest.
